inline_comments.message:
I do not see those files. Are they missing from the review?
Also, please pay attention that if these files are large, they will cause all onap helm chart to be too large to be deployed (due to k8s 1Mb limitation of configMap).

better add something like:
  portName2: clamp-ssl
We are not advancing chart versions just yet. Let's keep it as 2.0.0, the change will be done later.
Sorry, I have forgot to add the files. I will update the PL.
But for oom/clamp, I have already update the version to 3.0.0. If keep it 2.0.0 here, it means we will not take the new oom/clamp at this moment, so the new oom/clamp will only be used later, right?

You are right in your comment that if in requirements.yaml there is clamp 2.0.0, it will not take this version of clamp chart.
Therefore, I think for now clamp should stay at version 2.0.0, until we change all charts to version 3.0.0
We will discuss this in the weekly meeting.
Meanwhile, for sake of consistency, until global decision, let's keep it 2.0.0?

The 3.0.0 changes are implemented to cooperate with the Clamp Casablanca changes, which have AAF integrated. 2.0.0 is for Clamp Beijing release, which uses another way for user authentication/authorization. The 2 version shall not be mixed up, otherwise it will not work. That's why we want to put all the changes in 3.0.0. 
Maybe we can just keep it 3.0.0 here. And of course this version will not be used until the requirements.yaml is updated. What do you think?

I just noticed that this file size is huge (in oom standrards).
It will cause onap helm chart not to be able to be deployed due to k8s configMap size restriction of 1Mb for the whole chart.

A solution maybe similar to what is done in aaf 
(see files: aaf/templates/job.yaml, aaf/resources/config/public/README.txt)

Port 89 is already taken.
appc/charts/appc-cdt/values.yaml:  nodePort: 89
Is 34 ok? I have checked https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+NodePort+List , and also try to grep the nodePort under oom, 34 seems to be not used at this moment.
